meaning: n.低沉连续的声音,咕哝,怨言,低语v.发低沉连续的声音,发怨言,低声说,低语
examples: The doctor said James had now developed a heart murmur.
The excited murmur grew louder as the whole group collected in the old dining room.
She answered in a faint murmur.
Two well-heeled Japanese housewives murmur over their cups of coffee, while Mozart plays in the background.
In this one the contestants invoke Voltaire on the streets and murmur sweet nothings in bed.
